one_time_description: Prior period restatement adjustments related to reclassification of investments, purchase price premiums, and consolidation of Lower Kootenay Guide Outfitters Ltd.
economic_notes: First Nation operates multiple business enterprises including Ainsworth Hot Springs Ltd., Ainsworth Hot Springs Limited Partnership, Ka'amaknala Development Corporation, Lower Kootenay Development Limited Partnership, Lower Kootenay Development Corporation, Morris Flowers Inc., and yaqan nukiy Farms LP. Net loss from business enterprises of $716,449 in 2021 compared to income of $413,614 in 2020.
notes: Financial statements are restated comparative figures for 2020 due to: reclassification of Ktunaxa Holdings LP from GBE to long-term investment at cost; recording of purchase price premiums for Ainsworth Hot Springs Ltd. and Morris Flowers Inc.; correction of amortization in Ainsworth Hot Springs Ltd. and Lower Kootenay Guide Outfitters Ltd.; and retroactive consolidation of Lower Kootenay Guide Outfitters Ltd. Population stated as 'over 200 members'. No schedule of remuneration provided. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($2,365,421) + FNESC ($211,765) + FNHA ($2,368,625) + Province of BC ($1,668,768) + CMHC ($316,761) = $6,931,340. OSR includes rental income, other income, Ktunaxa Nation Council funding, and loss from business enterprises. Ktunaxa Nation Council ($2,500,833) treated as non-government transfer. Funds held in trust of $515,440 for band members land settlement are underfunded by $444,387 and not on balance sheet.
